ending: The book ends with the road trip abandoned and the family back home. The journey Greg's mother envisioned - spontaneous, wholesome, and bonding - collapsed under a live pig won at a county fair, a seagull invasion of the van, a dismal motel, a running feud with a bearded family they kept encountering, and an accumulating toll on the van and the family's belongings. The Heffleys never reached the destinations they talked about; they simply ran out of trip and drove home. Even so, the shared catalogue of disasters leaves the family closer than when they set out, and Manny's pig is kept as a household pet - a souvenir that outlasts the vacation itself into the later books.
in_short: Greg's mother packs the family into the van for a spontaneous, screen-free summer road trip with no fixed destination, and nearly everything about it goes wrong: Manny wins a live pig at a county fair that then rides along with them, seagulls invade the van after a snack, a night is spent in a dismal motel, and the family keeps crossing paths with a bearded rival family. The second half of the trip adds more mishaps with the van and their belongings until the itinerary is abandoned and the Heffleys head home. The planned destinations are never reached, but the shared ordeal draws the family closer, and the pig comes home with them as a pet.

recaps.text: Previously: Greg Heffley's journals have chronicled his middle-school years and family life - his schemes, his best friend Rowley, and the daily friction with his brothers Rodrick and Manny under the eye of his parents Frank and Susan. This volume leaves school behind entirely: it is summer, and the whole family is about to get into the van.

At Greg's mother's urging, the Heffleys set off on a spontaneous summer road trip with no fixed destination, meant to be a screen-free bonding adventure. It goes wrong almost immediately. A stop at a county fair ends with Manny winning a live pig, which joins the family in the van; an open window and a snack food invite a swarm of hungry seagulls into the car; and a night in a grim motel caps the early leg. Along the way the family keeps crossing paths with a bearded family whose repeated appearances become a running feud. The trip Greg's mother imagined and the trip they are actually on have already parted company.

The second half of the trip piles on further disasters - mishaps at the stops Greg's mother insists on, mounting trouble with the van and their belongings, and more run-ins with the bearded family - until the itinerary is abandoned and the Heffleys limp home. The destinations were never reached and almost nothing went to plan, but the shared ordeal leaves the family oddly closer than when they left, and the pig Manny won at the fair stays on as a household pet. The book ends with the trip over, the family home, and normal life resuming.
